rewritten_requirements: The system shall allow a plane object in the state ready for use (1) to accept the event «M» assign plane without changing its state.
The system shall allow a plane object in the state scheduled for maintenance (2) to accept the event «M» complete flight and transition to the state in maintenance (5).
The system shall prevent a plane object in the state scheduled for maintenance (2) from accepting the event «M» assign flight.
The system shall inform the user if assigning a plane fails.
The system shall allow a plane object to transition from the state in maintenance (2) to the state ready for use (1) only through the event «M» plane available.
The system shall allow a plane object in the state in maintenance (5) to transition out of that state only through the events «M» plane available or «M» plane withdrawn.

The system shall only allow the following events to affect a plane object: «M» plane ordered, «M» plane delivered, «M» plane available, «M» plane not available, «M» assign plane, «M» complete flight, «M» scheduling plane for maintenance, and «M» plane withdrawn.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: A plane object in the state ready for use (1) accepts the event «M» assign plane and remains in the state ready for use (1).
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: The event is allowed if a transition (an arrow) to another state exists that is labeled with the event name, or if an internal transition exists.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: A plane object in the state scheduled for maintenance (2) accepts the event «M» complete flight and transitions to the state in maintenance (5), since no more flights are assigned to it.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: We have to check if there are more flights assigned to the plane. In our case we assume that no more flights are assigned to the plane.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: A plane object in the state scheduled for maintenance (2) does not accept the event «M» assign flight.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: In our example, neither a transition to another state nor an internal transition exists.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: The IT system should inform the user about the reason why assigning the plane did not work.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: (The IT system should inform the user about the reason why assigning the plane did not work.)

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: A plane object can only reach the state ready for use (1) though the event «M» plane available (namely, from the state in maintenance (2)).
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: Our example has exactly one such transition.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: A plane object in the state in maintenance (5) can only leave this state through the event «M» plane available, or «M» plane withdrawn.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: This means: A plane object in the state in maintenance (5) can only leave this state through the event «M» plane available, or «M» plane withdrawn.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: The only events relevant for a plane object are «M» plane ordered, «M» plane delivered, «M» plane available, «M» plane not available, «M» assign plane, «M» complete flight, «M» scheduling plane for maintenance, and «M» plane withdrawn.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: This means: The only events relevant for a plane object are «M» plane ordered, «M» plane delivered, «M» plane available, «M» plane not available, «M» assign plane, «M» complete flight, «M» scheduling plane for maintenance, and «M» plane withdrawn.
